states.name: Voraxion
states.title: Adamantine Maw
states.description:
Long, long ago, a destitute kingdom fell under siege. Fearing his people's poverty would mean their downfall, the king commissioned a group of alchemists to craft him an endless source of gold.

After some months, the alchemists presented him with a tiny beast. In front of him, they fed it scrap metal, spoiled food, all manner of the kingdom's waste -- and he watched as horns of solid gold grew on its body.

The king wept for joy, for with this monster, he'd saved his kingdom.

Or so he thought.

states.name: Voraxion
states.title: Adamantine Maw
states.description:
Months passed. The relentless war continued, and even with the gold from the beast's severed horns, the kingdom could not push the enemy back. Desperate, the king begged the alchemists to push the creature further. To feed it more and more. To curse it with hunger so great, it could never stop eating.

The alchemists warned the king that such a curse could have consequences more dire than any war, but he wouldn't listen. They'd given this monster life themselves. Surely, if it ever turned on them, they could force it back into servitude.

states.name: Voraxion
states.title: Adamantine Maw
states.description:
The alchemists obediently gave the creature a hunger no living thing has ever known. With every anguished day, its body shifted and grew, until it had to be chained down with its own gold, the one thing it could not consume.

In the end, the king's gambit paid off. The beast's torture earned him enough gold to win his war. When the enemy messenger arrived with terms of surrender, however, he found a sinkhole in place of a palace.

The monster could not consume its chains, it was true. But it had grown blades on its arms to break them.
